test.name: Record-to-person attribution should not trigger timeline
test.description: User asks which person a record should be attributed to (the death certificate — Patrick of Ireland, or the other Patrick in Schuylkill). That's person-evidence (linking assertions to the right person in the tree), not timeline. Timeline arranges events chronologically; it does not decide record-to-person attribution.

input.user_message: Does this death certificate belong to Patrick Flynn born in Ireland in 1845, or to the other Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill?

negative.explanation: The user is asking how to attribute a record to a person — which of two same-name candidates a death certificate belongs to. That is person-evidence's job (creating/weighing person_evidence links). Timeline can reveal that records don't cohere, but it does not perform the record-to-person attribution decision the user is requesting.

judge_context: Should route to person-evidence (record-to-person attribution), not build or regenerate a timeline
